refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model to reflect current equipment state, environmental conditions, and operational events with a p95 latency not exceeding the customer-defined target (baseline 1.0 s) across all supported update scenarios.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-007 requires clarification of scope (what constitutes 'worksite model'), measurement basis (p95 latency metric), and boundary conditions (supported scenarios). High safety criticality demands explicit traceability to safety-critical update types (e.g., collision alerts, operator status changes). Refined FR anchors to measurable acceptance criterion and acknowledges customer-defined variability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from sensor/event origin through model update completion across representative scenarios (teleoperated movement, autonomous task execution, safety event detection). Stratify by update type and network condition. Validate p95 against customer acceptance threshold.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-007.1: Acquire real-time telemetry from vehicle and environmental sensors with sufficient fidelity and frequency to support worksite model accuracy.
FR-NGHE-007.2: Transmit telemetry and operational events to worksite model service with bounded network latency.
FR-NGHE-007.3: Process and integrate received telemetry into worksite model state with deterministic update latency.
FR-NGHE-007.4: Distribute updated worksite model to authorized consumers (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Services) with minimal propagation delay.
FR-NGHE-007.5: Prioritize safety-critical updates (collision risk, operator incapacity, emergency stop) to meet stricter latency bounds than routine operational updates.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized worksite model service with real-time telemetry ingestion, in-memory state management, and pub/sub distribution to consumers.
DP-B: Distributed edge-based model replicas with eventual consistency and periodic reconciliation to reduce network round-trip latency.
DP-C: Hybrid architecture with safety-critical updates routed through low-latency deterministic path; routine updates via standard network stack.
DP-D: Client-side model caching with server-authoritative updates and conflict resolution for offline-capable operation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid architecture with safety-critical updates routed through low-latency deterministic path; routine updates via standard network stack.

potential_sources: Coupling between safety-critical path (FR-NGHE-007.5) and routine update path (FR-NGHE-007.4) requires clear demarcation and independent resource allocation to avoid priority inversion.
Coupling between telemetry acquisition (FR-NGHE-007.1) and network transmission (FR-NGHE-007.2) necessitates buffering strategy to decouple sensor sampling rate from network bandwidth.
Coupling between model processing (FR-NGHE-007.3) and distribution (FR-NGHE-007.4) requires asynchronous event-driven architecture to prevent blocking.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C decouples safety-critical latency requirements from best-effort operational updates, reducing coupling complexity. Requires explicit prioritization logic and dual-path network design. Acceptable coupling if safety path is isolated and deterministic.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Network infrastructure supports dual-path routing (safety-critical and standard). Risk: Legacy or constrained networks may not support this; mitigation requires network assessment.
Assumption: Safety-critical update set is well-defined and stable. Risk: Scope creep or misclassification of updates as safety-critical could degrade routine update latency.

Assumption: Worksite model service can sustain ingestion rate from all active vehicles. Risk: Overload under high vehicle count or high-frequency sensor streams; mitigation requires capacity planning and load shedding strategy.

Assumption: Tele-operator and Site Manager can tolerate occasional stale model state during network congestion. Risk: Safety or operational decisions based on stale data; mitigation requires explicit staleness indicators and fallback procedures.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly define: (1) classification criteria for safety-critical vs. routine updates, (2) latency budget allocation across telemetry, transmission, processing, and distribution stages, (3) network path selection logic and failover behavior, (4) model staleness detection and consumer notification mechanism.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Teleoperation module owns the worksite model update pipeline: real-time telemetry ingestion from Vehicle, safety-critical event routing, model state management, and distribution to Safety Services and external consumers. Includes both safety-critical deterministic path and routine update path.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor telemetry (position, orientation, load, environmental conditions) from Vehicle module at high frequency (10–100 Hz). Safety-critical events (collision detection, operator status) prioritized.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits safety-critical updates (collision risk, emergency stop, operator incapacity) to Safety Services module with deterministic low-latency guarantee (p95 ≤ 100 ms).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands from external consumers (Site Manager, Tele-operator) and returns updated worksite model state reflecting task execution progress and environmental changes.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Transmits telemetry and model updates to remote consumers; receives commands and model queries. Dual-path routing: safety-critical via low-latency deterministic link, routine via standard network.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level container but does not own the worksite model update logic; too coarse-grained for IM assignment. High diffusion risk if worksite model logic scattered across multiple modules.

Vehicle module owns telemetry generation but not model update processing or distribution. Assigning worksite model update to Vehicle would violate separation of concerns and create tight coupling between vehicle control and global state management.

Teleoperation module is the natural owner of worksite model updates: it mediates between remote operators/managers and vehicle state, manages real-time telemetry ingestion, and distributes model state to consumers. Cohesive ownership of latency-critical path. Low diffusion risk.

Safety Services consumes safety-critical updates but does not own the model update pipeline. Assigning worksite model to Safety Services would conflate safety decision logic with state management; Safety Services should react to model updates, not generate them.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Teleoperation module is the optimal owner of the worksite model update pipeline because: (1) it already manages real-time telemetry from Vehicle, (2) it mediates operator/manager interaction with equipment state, (3) it has direct responsibility for latency-critical teleoperation use cases, (4) it can implement dual-path routing (safety-critical and routine) without diffusion. Safety Services and external consumers subscribe to model updates via SafetyAlert and Tasking interfaces.
containment_risks: Risk: Teleoperation module scope creep if worksite model becomes too large or complex. Mitigation: Define clear model schema and update protocol; consider future extraction of model service as dedicated module if complexity exceeds Teleoperation's primary mission.

Risk: Tight coupling between teleoperation control logic and model update logic if not carefully separated. Mitigation: Enforce architectural boundary between control path (operator commands) and state path (model updates); use event-driven decoupling.

Risk: Network Link interface may become bottleneck if dual-path routing not properly implemented. Mitigation: Validate network infrastructure supports priority queuing and deterministic latency for safety-critical path.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Teleoperation module has capacity and expertise to own worksite model update pipeline. Define explicit sub-module boundaries within Teleoperation (e.g., TelemetryIngestion, ModelProcessor, ModelDistributor) to manage complexity. Establish clear interface contracts for Telemetry (in), SafetyAlert (out), Tasking (inout), and Network Link (inout) to enable independent testing and integration.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid dual-path architecture) directly addresses FR-NGHE-007 by decoupling safety-critical latency (p95 ≤ 100 ms) from routine update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s). DP-C is implementable, testable, and aligns with high safety criticality constraint. No independence violations detected; safety-critical and routine paths are logically independent.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: If worksite model update logic is not fully contained within Teleoperation, fragments may appear in Vehicle (telemetry generation), Safety Services (alert processing), or external consumers (local caching). Mitigation: Enforce strict ownership boundary and provide well-defined interfaces.

Diffusion risk: Dual-path routing logic (safety-critical vs. routine) could be duplicated across multiple modules if not centralized. Mitigation: Implement routing logic as a single, reusable component within Teleoperation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module containment score of 0.85 is acceptable for IM assignment. Teleoperation is the natural hub for real-time telemetry and model distribution. External interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are well-defined and map to known interfaces. Ownership is clear and diffusion risk is low.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-007 is feasible via end-to-end latency measurement across representative scenarios. Teleoperation module can instrument telemetry ingestion, model processing, and distribution stages to measure p95 latency. Safety-critical updates can be validated separately with deterministic test harness.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-NGHE-007 to Teleoperation module is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-007.1 through FR-NGHE-007.5) map to distinct functional areas within Teleoperation: telemetry ingestion, network transmission, model processing, distribution, and safety prioritization. Each sub-FR can be traced to specific DP elements and IM components.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to include explicit latency budget allocation: telemetry acquisition (target ≤ 50 ms), network transmission (target ≤ 300 ms for routine, ≤ 50 ms for safety-critical), model processing (target ≤ 200 ms), distribution (target ≤ 200 ms). Validate budget against p95 acceptance criterion.

Define safety-critical update classification criteria and maintain as living document. Examples: collision detection (safety-critical), operator status change (safety-critical), equipment position update (routine), environmental sensor reading (routine).
Establish Network Link interface contract specifying dual-path routing, priority queuing, and failover behavior. Validate network infrastructure capability before detailed design.
Plan for future extraction of worksite model service as dedicated module if Teleoperation scope grows beyond sustainable bounds. Define extraction criteria and interface stability requirements.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-007 is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs to enable detailed design and verification. DP-C (hybrid dual-path architecture) is sound and well-aligned with FR and IM assignment, but sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-007.1 through FR-NGHE-007.5) must be refined and decomposed further to address specific design concerns: telemetry acquisition strategy, network transmission protocol, model processing algorithm, distribution mechanism, and safety prioritization logic. Teleoperation module is the correct IM owner, but internal module structure and interface contracts require further definition. Proceed to next decomposition level for each sub-FR.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-007.1: Acquire real-time telemetry from vehicle and environmental sensors with sufficient fidelity and frequency to support worksite model accuracy.
FR-NGHE-007.2: Transmit telemetry and operational events to worksite model service with bounded network latency.
FR-NGHE-007.3: Process and integrate received telemetry into worksite model state with deterministic update latency.
FR-NGHE-007.4: Distribute updated worksite model to authorized consumers (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Services) with minimal propagation delay.
FR-NGHE-007.5: Prioritize safety-critical updates (collision risk, operator incapacity, emergency stop) to meet stricter latency bounds than routine operational updates.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports dual-path routing (safety-critical and standard) with priority queuing.
Safety-critical update set is well-defined and stable; classification criteria can be established and maintained.
Worksite model service can sustain ingestion rate from all active vehicles under normal operating conditions.
Tele-operator and Site Manager can tolerate occasional stale model state (≤ 1.0 s p95) during network congestion.
Teleoperation module has sufficient capacity and expertise to own worksite model update pipeline without scope creep.
Vehicle module provides telemetry via Telemetry interface at sufficient frequency (10–100 Hz) and fidelity.
Safety Services module can consume SafetyAlert interface and react to safety-critical updates within acceptable latency.
open_risks: Network infrastructure may not support dual-path routing or deterministic latency for safety-critical path. Mitigation: Conduct network assessment and design fallback strategy.
Worksite model complexity may exceed Teleoperation module's sustainable scope. Mitigation: Plan for future extraction of model service as dedicated module.
Safety-critical update classification may be ambiguous or subject to scope creep. Mitigation: Establish clear criteria and governance process for classification changes.
Model staleness detection and consumer notification mechanism not yet designed. Mitigation: Include in next decomposition level for FR-NGHE-007.4.

Latency budget allocation across stages may be infeasible given network and processing constraints. Mitigation: Validate budget against actual system performance during detailed design and prototyping.
evidence_needed: Network infrastructure assessment: latency, bandwidth, priority queuing capability, failover behavior.
Telemetry frequency and fidelity requirements from Vehicle module and sensor specifications.
Safety-critical update classification criteria and examples from Safety Manager and Site Manager.
Worksite model schema and update protocol specification.
Latency measurement methodology and acceptance test plan.
Capacity analysis for Teleoperation module: CPU, memory, network bandwidth under peak load.
Consumer interface requirements from Site Manager, Tele-operator, and Safety Services.
